hero.title: The business case for U.S. expansion.
hero.subtitle: The entire project in a single, navigable document. Data, scenarios and roadmap across nine pages.

kpi.title: The business case in four dimensions.

market.label: U.S. pet food market 2025
market.caption: Total pet food market 2025. Premium/holistic segment +12–15% annually.

breakeven.label: Break-even, base scenario
breakeven.caption: Recalibrated base scenario with scaled MD salary and 5 pre-validated clients.

index.title: Browse the dossier by decision area.

supplyChain.title: The Italy — New Jersey bridge.
supplyChain.subtitle: A supply chain already in place: from the Marche region to the Port of Newark, 25-38 days transit time.

transit.label: Total transit time
transit.note: From Marche departure to Linden receiving

supplyChain.bottomNote: No production to launch in the U.S., no dependence on third-party suppliers. The supply chain already exists in Italy — we extend it across the ocean.

hero.title: The U.S. market and the Made in Italy window.
hero.subtitle: $136 billion in 2025, premium/holistic growing double-digit, zero Italian brands positioned.
hero.source: APPA 2025 · Packaged Facts 2021-2025 · Appendix 03 — Market Analysis

kpi.households: U.S. households with at least one pet

segments.title: Premium and holistic are growing double-digit.
segments.subtitle: Alleva's target segment is accelerating faster than the overall market.

channels.title: E-commerce has overtaken everything else combined.
channels.subtitle: Channel market share 2015 → 2025. A clear shift toward online.

actions.ecommerce: Seller Central setup month 4-5, launch month 6

chewy.strength: Best-in-class customer service, autoship drives 75% of revenue

dtc.strength: Made in Italy brand story, 100% margin

competitors.title: No Italian brand, five North American competitors.
competitors.subtitle: Comparison on key parameters: price, protein, origin, channel, differentiators.
competitors.positioning: $23–26 retail · 27–46% protein · Taurine 4000 mg (cat) · Grain-free 7/8 SKUs · Functional ingredients (Aloe, Ginseng, Hemp) · Made in Italy

trends.title: Six trends working in Alleva's favor.

grainFree.title: Grain-free on the rise
grainFree.impact: 7 of 8 Alleva SKUs are grain-free — perfect alignment.

singleProtein.title: Single-protein preference
singleProtein.impact: Ocean Fish dog & cat ideal for sensitive pets.

functional.title: Functional ingredients
functional.impact: Aloe, Ginseng, Hemp, Green Lipped Mussel — USPs that can't be replicated.

madeInItaly.title: Made in Italy traceability
madeInItaly.impact: Unique competitive advantage vs generic U.S. brands.

taurine.title: Post-FDA DCM taurine awareness
taurine.impact: 4000 mg/kg in cat formulas — a strong selling argument (the FDA has flagged DCM risk linked to taurine deficiency).

humanization.title: Humanization and premiumization
humanization.impact: 67% of U.S. households are spending more on their pets. A secular trend, not a cyclical one.

geography.title: New Jersey is the natural launchpad.
geography.subtitle: High income, a 1.5M-strong Italian community, direct access to the port.

whitespace.text: No competitor offers the combination: Made in Italy + grain-free + functional ingredients + superior protein and taurine levels. This is the white space Diusapet occupies — and first-mover advantage here is worth about three years of lead time that others can't replicate.

hero.title: Eight SKUs to open the market.
hero.subtitle: Four dog, four cat. All with 41–47% gross margin and premium protein levels. Phase 1 launches four SKUs with the highest expected rotation.

differentiators.title: Why the positioning holds against U.S. premium brands.

closing.title: Four SKUs to start, eight at steady state.
closing.text: Phase 1 launches Puppy Chicken&Duck, Adult Chicken&Duck, Adult Natural Chicken&Potato and Ocean Fish for dogs — the highest-rotation references. Cat arrives at Month 6 with Kitten, Adult, Ocean Fish and Light.

hero.title: One MD, one operator, one flow.
hero.subtitle: 4,000 sqft in Linden NJ, manual bagging, direct deliveries. MD-operated for the first 12 months.
hero.phaseBadge: Phase 1 — MD as sole operator · 600 bags/month

allocation.title: 40% of the week is in the van.
allocation.subtitle: {hours}h/week allocated by operating category. Rest is excluded from the bar because it is not productive work time.

cadence.title: Beyond the week: the recurring frame.

callout.textMain: This model holds up to 800 bags/month.
callout.textSub: Beyond that, Phase 2 kicks in: a part-time operator + dedicated driver.

warehouse.title: 4,000 sqft, seven zones, a single flow.
warehouse.subtitle: 4,000 sqft, 7 zones, one operator. A linear flow from container to dispatch.

details.hoverHint: Hover a zone to see details

allocation.title: 68% is productive, 32% is aisles.
allocation.subtitle: Aisles and maneuvering space are required for pallet jack operation and OSHA compliance. 68% is typical efficiency for an operator-run warehouse.

corridor.label: 1,275 sqft aisles and maneuvering
corridor.note: Required for pallet jack + OSHA safety

setup.title: Four thousand dollars to start.
setup.capacityNote: At 600 bags/month (Year 1 base case), 4–6 hours of bagging per week are enough. The layout scales up to 1,500 bags/month without modifications. Beyond that, Phase 2 activates with a part-time operator.

includes.storage: Heavy-duty shelving + pallet rack + pallet jack
includes.bagging: Heat sealer + scale + stainless table + hopper
includes.safety: Fire extinguishers + signage + PPE + first aid kit

mdWeek.title: 55-60 hours a week, 40% behind the wheel.
mdWeek.subtitle: The MD is production, sales, logistics and customer relationship all at once.
mdWeek.vanStrip: 40% of time in the van (NJ + NY + PA)
mdWeek.monthlyNote: Monthly cadence: 2× flight to Italy (kids), 1× Global Pet Expo in February.

hero.title: Recalibrated numbers, transparent scenarios.
hero.subtitle: Three scenarios modeled over 36 months. Five recalibration levers push the feasibility score from 70 to 82.
hero.recalibrationNote: The plan is recalibrated against the original dossier by applying five operational levers that improve feasibility from 70/100 to 82/100 without changing the business model.

kpi.capexCaption: Legal setup, first inventory, 4-month buffer, contingency.
kpi.opexCaption: Lean team, 3PL Linden, trade marketing, compliance.

simulator.title: Pessimistic, base, optimistic — with what-if overrides.
simulator.note: The scenario sets the structural assumptions. The sliders apply ±20% on volume and ±10% on price for what-if testing.

levers.title: Five operational levers that shift the plan from 70/100 to 82/100.

salary.title: Tiered MD compensation
salary.desc: $8,000/month for the first 6 months (pre-revenue and ramp-up), then $12,000/month at steady state. Reduces Y1 load while keeping a dignified compensation from day 1.

preSold.title: 5 pre-signed clients
preSold.desc: Close 5 signed orders before shipping the first container. Validates demand and allows a smaller initial order.

reducedSku.title: Launch with 4 SKUs + half container
reducedSku.desc: Phase 1 focused on the 4 highest-margin SKUs (>45%) and a half-container first order. Reduces initial capex and inventory risk.
reducedSku.risk: Low — remaining 4 SKUs enter in Phase 2

warehouse.title: Warehouse Linden vs Carteret
warehouse.desc: Linden/Avenel at $13/sqft chosen over Carteret at $16/sqft. Structural delta that compounds year over year.
warehouse.risk: Low — both are suitable industrial zones

inHouse.title: In-house manual bagging, Phase 1
inHouse.desc: MD runs the impulse sealer (~600 bags/month). Zero additional labor cost, capex limited to $4k of equipment.

chart.title: From month one to operating break-even.

feasibility.title: Eight dimensions assessed, seven improving.
feasibility.explanation: The score is the weighted average of the 8 operating dimensions of the plan. The delta reflects the effect of the 5 applied levers only.

comments.breakeven: Month 27 → Month 20 thanks to the 5 levers

hero.title: 106 prospects across NJ/NY/PA — one counts twice.
hero.subtitle: CRM synced with Cowork. One UNICUM: Alleva Dairy in Lyndhurst, same surname as the brand family.

map.title: The tri-state area, name by name.
map.description: High density across Hudson County (Jersey City, Hoboken) and Brooklyn (Bay Ridge): the MD's daily corridor. Each dot is a real prospect; clusters of ≥3 are aggregated with the count inside. Click a marker to jump to the record.

cta.title: 5 signed orders from the Top HOT.

hero.subtitle: Two reading levels: the first 90 days at weekly granularity, the 36-month horizon at strategic level.

criticalPath.title: The six steps that cannot slip.
criticalPath.note: If one slips, the whole container slips with it. The E-2 visa is the longest chain: 6 weeks of consulate wait.

phaseNotes.launch: Warehouse live, first container, first sales
phaseNotes.growth: Zone 2 expansion, Amazon live, Global Pet Expo
phaseNotes.consolidate: Phase 2, full-time operator, CT + upstate NY

callout.text: The project is won or lost in the first 90 days. Once the first container lands in NJ, the rest is repeated execution.

hero.title: Risks and mitigations.
hero.subtitle: Five monitored risks, six quantified stress tests, one calibrated liquidity buffer.
hero.source: Appendix 13 — Final Report · Appendix 10-12 — Scenarios and Sensitivity

matrix.title: The five principal risks, named.
matrix.subtitle: Each with estimated probability, impact and an operational response plan.

visa.name: E-2 visa delay or denial
visa.desc: The consulate may request additional documentation or deny the application on business-plan technicalities.
visa.response: E-2 specialist attorney from week 1. Flawless documentation. Plan B: L-1 / O-1 alternative visas. Plan C: temporary remote management with a U.S. partner.

sales.name: Sales below 50% of forecast
sales.desc: Commercial penetration slower than expected in the first 6–9 months.
sales.response: $30k liquidity buffer covers 6 extra months of fixed costs. MD compensation is guaranteed by the plan. Lease break clause at month 18 as exit option. Accelerate Amazon and distributor channels.

fda.name: FDA shipment hold
fda.desc: FDA inspection at customs with detain/hold/refuse — may delay or compromise the container.
fda.response: Experienced FSVP consultant from month 3. Flawless Prior Notice on every shipment. Reduced first container (half load) as pilot. Samples tested in an independent laboratory.

competitor.name: Italian competitor enters the market
competitor.desc: Another Italian brand (e.g. Farmina, Monge) occupies the Made in Italy space before we do.
competitor.response: First-mover advantage + already-acquired NJ Italian community = an advantage that can't be replicated in under 3 years. Accelerate exclusivity contracts with Top 15 prospects. U.S. trademark filings on the brand.

zoning.name: Warehouse zoning not suitable
zoning.desc: The target lot (e.g. Carteret) may be zoned retail rather than industrial / light manufacturing.
zoning.response: Municipal verification MANDATORY before signing. Alternative options already identified: Linden, Avenel, Elizabeth, Rahway. No signature without zoning certificate.

stress.eyebrow: SIX STRESS TESTS QUANTIFIED ON YEAR 2 EBITDA
stress.title: Six quantified what-ifs.
stress.subtitle: Every scenario is modeled with dollar impact and a management plan.

fdaDelay.manageability: $30k buffer + 4-month fixed-cost reserve cover the event

levers.title: Four mitigation levers live from Day 1.

buffer.name: Liquidity buffer
buffer.note: Additional reserve beyond the 4 months of fixed costs already in the investment. Covers 6 extra months if sales slow.

breakClause.name: Lease break clause at month 18
breakClause.note: Contractual clause negotiated with the landlord at signing. Provides the option to close operations with limited cost if the pessimistic scenario materializes.

halfContainer.name: Reduced first container (half load)
halfContainer.note: Instead of 2 full containers, Phase 1 ships with a half-load of the 4 highest-margin SKUs. Reduces FDA and inventory exposure.

preSold.name: 5 pre-signed orders before container
preSold.note: Target: 5 signed orders from Top 15 prospects before shipping. Validates demand and protects against slow-penetration risk.

feasibility.title: From 70/100 to 82/100 through the five levers.
feasibility.subtitle: The recalibrated plan moves the feasibility score by 12 points without altering the business model.

decalogue.title: Ten principles for realistic expectations.
decalogue.subtitle: Ten principles to read the project without illusions and without alarm.

items.i1: The business will not be profitable (MD compensation included) for the first 2 years — this is normal and expected.
items.i2: The MD is paid from Day 1. Compensation is in the plan, not a risk to worry about later.
items.i3: Operational break-even (excluding compensation) at Month 9 with 800 bags/month — proof the model works.
items.i4: $185k total investment is among the lowest possible for an operation of this type.
items.i5: The main risk is slow commercial penetration — mitigated by the MD's own selling.
items.i6: Value created is not only EBITDA: it's the brand, the customer base, the LLC — all monetizable later.
items.i7: The NJ Italian community is a real and non-replicable competitive advantage.
items.i8: The CARE line is a ready-to-launch growth option — no additional development cost.
items.i9: In the optimistic scenario (Global Pet Expo 2026), the entire investment is paid back in 18 months.
items.i10: It's worth it — adequate compensation, NJ lifestyle, kids reachable 2×/month, assets being built.

callout.text: The project is FEASIBLE. The numbers hold, the market is there, the supply chain is ready. The question is not WHETHER to do it, but WHEN — and the answer is now, before someone else occupies the Made in Italy space in U.S. pet food.

hero.subtitle: Every chapter of the business plan and every analytical appendix, downloadable in Word.

allegati.footerNote: Files are placeholders. Wiring to the private bucket will be enabled at deployment on usa.diusapet.work.

followUp.title: 80% of conversions come from follow-ups.
followUp.subtitle: The first email gets a 2–3% reply rate. Every follow-up adds percentage points. You do not stop after the first.
followUp.insight: Counter-intuitive: the break-up email is often the highest-converting one. It removes pressure, creates reciprocity, and triggers loss aversion ("wait, give me two more days").

t0.name: Initial email
t0.angle: Personalized hook + value prop + sample CTA

t5.name: Follow-up 1
t5.angle: Reminder of context + new social proof + soft CTA

t12.name: Follow-up 2
t12.angle: Change angle (if technical first → emotional now)

t22.name: Break-up email
t22.angle: "I understand it is not the right time" — closes the loop

t90.name: Cold re-engage
t90.angle: News / product updates after a quarter

templates.title: Seven angles, seven templates.
templates.subtitle: Every type of contact has its own angle. No generic emails.

A.name: Italian heritage angle
A.when: Contacts with an Italian surname (D'Elia, Longo, Rizzo), stores in Italian neighborhoods (Edison, Hoboken, Bay Ridge), stated Italian-American customer base.

B.name: Holistic / nutrition angle
B.when: Stores carrying Orijen, Acana, Fromm, Wellness Core; nutrition-literate owners.

C.name: Orijen-shelf angle
C.target: Stores that carry Orijen or Acana
C.when: Stores already selling Canadian premium; pitch: same shelf, better margin, Made in Italy story.

D.name: Cat specialty angle
D.target: Cat specialty stores and vet channel
D.when: Cat-only stores, vets with pet retail; pitch: 4,000 mg/kg taurine, feline cardiac health focus.

E.name: Italian breed angle
E.when: AKC-certified breeders; 200+ bags/month volume; emotional Made in Italy pitch.

F.name: Italian specialty store angle
F.when: Stores with an Italian food section; cross-sell pitch with Italophile customer base.

G.name: Distributor angle
G.when: Regional distributors (Zeigler, Fauna); pitch: margin, territory exclusivity, marketing support.

subjects.title: Subject is 70% of the game.
subjects.subtitle: Nobody opens "Introducing Alleva." They open subjects with a number, a benefit, or a legitimate hook.

introducing.reason: Reads like a press release, <5% opens

tooLong.reason: Gets cut off on mobile (70% of buyers read on phone)

cta.title: The three CTAs that convert.
cta.subtitle: The most effective CTA asks for something so small that saying "no" would feel impolite.

freeSample.name: Free sample pack
freeSample.use: Primary CTA for every pet store and Italian deli

replyAnswer.name: Reply instead of apply
replyAnswer.use: Follow-up T+12 — opens the door to an easy no

numbers.title: Three non-negotiable numbers.
numbers.subtitle: If an email doesn't contain at least one of these, it isn't selling Alleva.

taurine.label: taurine
taurine.useWith: Cat specialty, vet channel, cat breeders

activities: Decide priorities: scouting zone + email batch
Launch scouting for the chosen zone
Generate a batch of 15–20 HOT emails
Send in the same thread (reply, not new email)
Calls to the top 5 HOT who haven't replied
+40% response rate after 2 emails and 1 call

open.ifBelow: Weak subject lines — revisit the pattern

positive.ifBelow: Value prop not aligned with the profile

velocity.formula: Avg days from lead to order
velocity.ifBelow: If >90d: cycle too long, refine CTA

meta.title: How Diusapet USA fits into the group.
meta.subtitle: Three entities under a single holding. Commercial flows run horizontally between Italy and the U.S., while U.S. earnings stay in the U.S. until distribution.
meta.source: Appendix 05 — Corporate Feasibility · Bulgaria-Italy-U.S. tax structure

kpi.entitiesLabel: Diusa SA → SRL IT + LLC US

sa.function: Holds the operating stakes and capitalizes the U.S. LLC. Financial, governance and group strategy role.

srl.function: Alleva brand manufacturing. Owns the know-how, formulas and trademark. Supplies product to the U.S. LLC under Supply Agreement.

llc.function: Bagging, import and distribution on the U.S. market. Separate legal entity with limited liability.

hierarchy.title: One holding, two operating companies, dedicated flows.
hierarchy.subtitle: The Bulgarian SA holds 100% of both operating companies. Italy and the U.S. operate in parallel through formal commercial relationships.

flowsAnimation.title: The full cycle: order, invoice, payment, earnings.
flowsAnimation.subtitle: Animation of the commercial cycle between SRL Italy and LLC U.S., repeated with every container.

step1.label: Container order
step1.desc: The U.S. LLC orders bulk product and empty bags from the Italian SRL.

step2.label: Supply + invoice
step2.desc: The SRL produces and ships. Issues intercompany invoice at documented transfer price.

step3.label: Supply payment
step3.desc: The LLC pays the invoice to the SRL. Transfer price OECD-compliant.

step4.label: U.S. sale + earnings
step4.desc: The LLC sells on the U.S. market. Net earnings after U.S. taxes stay in the LLC for Phase 2 reinvestment.

dividend.title: U.S. earnings stay in the U.S. For now.
dividend.subtitle: In Phase 1-2 (years 1-3) earnings remain in the LLC to finance growth and working capital. Distribution to the holding starts only from Phase 3 onward.

phase1.note: All earnings reinvested in the LLC: working capital, Zone 2 expansion, semi-automated operations, marketing.

phase2.note: Annual dividends from the LLC to the Bulgarian SA. 5% withholding thanks to the U.S.-Bulgaria treaty.

dividend.taxNote: The Bulgaria-U.S. tax treaty provides a 5% withholding on dividends (vs 30% standard without treaty). Bulgaria has a favorable holding regime, with a 10% flat tax on income.
dividend.whyBulgaria: Diusa SA is the existing holding of the Diusa group. The Bulgaria choice was made before the U.S. project for reasons of tax efficiency and operational simplicity of the European holding.

roles.title: Group-level CEO, local operating MD.
roles.subtitle: Standard governance structure for foreign subsidiaries of European groups.

ceo.title: Chief Executive Officer
ceo.scope: Group-level governance, strategic approvals, framework contracts, relationship with the holding.
ceo.resp1: Legal representation of the LLC toward the Bulgarian holding and the Italian SRL
ceo.resp2: Approval of annual budget and of deviations above threshold
ceo.resp3: Signing of contracts with national distributors and strategic trade shows
ceo.resp4: Decisions on capital allocation, Phase 2-3 expansion, exit strategy

md.title: Managing Director
md.scope: Full P&L of the U.S. LLC. Operations, sales, compliance, local customer relationships.
md.resp1: Day-to-day management of the LLC: warehouse, manual production, deliveries
md.resp2: Commercial development: pet stores, Italian markets, breeders, distributors
md.resp3: FDA, FSVP and OSHA compliance at operating level
md.resp4: CRM management, commercial outreach, samples, follow-up
md.resp5: Monthly reporting to the group-level CEO and to the holding

decisionMatrix.title: Who decides what.
decisionMatrix.subtitle: Delegations and approval thresholds to be formalized in the LLC Operating Agreement.
decisionMatrix.d3: Commercial discounts above 10% or exclusivity contracts
decisionMatrix.d4: Geographic expansion into a new zone
decisionMatrix.d10: Exit, sale or restructuring of the LLC

contracts.title: Five formal relationships between entities.
contracts.subtitle: Every commercial and capital relationship is governed by a specific contract, required for OECD transfer pricing compliance.

capital.what: Establishment capital of the U.S. LLC: equity plus intercompany loan
capital.note: Ownership relationship. The SA holds 100% of the LLC from Day 1.

license.what: License to use the Alleva trademark on U.S. territory
license.note: Required for OECD transfer pricing compliance.

supply.what: Supply of bulk product (bigbag) and empty bags
supply.consideration: Transfer price at Italian industrial margin (€ per kg)
supply.note: The price is documented in the transfer pricing study.

services.note: Coverage for ongoing technical support from Italy.

dividends.what: Annual dividends on earnings after U.S. taxes
dividends.note: Distribution suspended in Phase 1-2. Active from Year 4.

r1.title: Limited liability
r1.text: The U.S. LLC protects the holding and the Italian SRL from potential U.S. liabilities: product liability, customer litigation, workplace injuries. Risk stays isolated to the capital contributed to the LLC.

r2.title: Tax optimization via SA
r2.text: Diusa SA in Bulgaria is the existing group holding. It enables a favorable tax regime on dividends received from operating entities (IT and U.S.) and a 10% Bulgarian flat tax on holding income.

r3.title: Scalable governance
r3.text: Separation between group-level CEO and country MD allows the LLC to grow without overloading the holding. The same structure is replicable if Diusapet Germany or UK open tomorrow.

r4.title: Credibility with U.S. stakeholders
r4.text: Banks, distributors and the FDA prefer dealing with a U.S. LLC run by a resident MD rather than with a branch of a foreign company. This simplifies compliance and account opening.

r5.title: Exit optionality
r5.text: Having the LLC as a separate entity enables a future sale of only the U.S. LLC, a joint venture with an American partner, an investor entry or a partial IPO. Options a branch office would not allow.

capital.title: How the U.S. LLC is capitalized.
capital.subtitle: The $185k investment is contributed by the Diusa SA holding as a combination of equity and intercompany loan.
capital.injectorRole: Holding that capitalizes the LLC directly

equity.note: Capital contribution from Diusa SA for LLC establishment. Required for the E-2 visa as evidence of substantial investment.

loan.note: Shareholder loan from Diusa SA at market rate (SOFR + 3%). Repayable from U.S. earnings over a 5-year horizon.

total.note: Aligned with the financial plan's total initial investment.

capital.visaNote: The 50k equity + 135k loan structure was designed to satisfy the E-2 visa requirements for the MD. USCIS requires the investment to be substantial and at risk: $50k of irreversible equity satisfies this test, while the loan provides operating liquidity without weighing down the equity.
capital.separationNote: This is the capital structure, meaning the ownership relationship. Diusa SA capitalizes the LLC because it owns 100% of it. Diusapet S.r.l. instead sells product to the LLC under a Supply Agreement: a distinct commercial relationship that has nothing to do with ownership.

closing.text: The SA-holding structure with group-level CEO and country-level MD is the standard governance for European groups with U.S. subsidiaries. It formalizes the decision-making perimeter, shields the holding from U.S. operating risk, and provides a scalable framework for the group's future growth.
